assets.name: Player Character

assets.name: Tornado
assets.prompt: massive tornado funnel viewed from above swirling vortex gray-brown debris cloud pixel art dramatic transparent background
assets.notes: Tornado vortex sprite, 128x128, generated via PixelLab pixflux

assets.name: Pre-Build Report: Milestone Cycle 7–11
assets.notes: Animated HTML pre-build report for M7–M11. Terminal feature readout, tech debt diff view, risk matrix, milestone cards with complexity ratings, Alex Chen + Jordan Rivera feedback, architecture improvements, brainstorm wall.

assets.name: Post-Build Report: Milestone Cycle 2–6
assets.notes: Animated HTML post-build retrospective for completed M2–M6 block. Progress tracker, bug severity badges, shipped vs deferred comparison, performance charts, dev+PM feedback, 7 lessons learned, next block preview.

assets.name: Pre-Build Report: Milestone Cycle 2–6
assets.notes: Animated HTML pre-build report for M2–M6 cycle. Includes bug tracker, milestone plan, UX review, game dev + PM feedback, risk assessment, and brainstorm ideas.

assets.name: Pre-Creation Design Report
assets.notes: Animated HTML report covering game concept, core loop, entities, milestone plan, risks, and future ideas — written before implementation began.

reports.summary: Completed the M2–M6 block with score system, endless mode, death recap, daily challenge URL seeds, and accessibility pass.
changes: Score formula: baseScore (300−elapsed) + survivorBonus (150×rescued) + healthBonus (100×HP)
Top-5 score leaderboard in WinScene alongside time leaderboard (localStorage stormtown_scores)
Endless survival mode: title screen button, escalates tornadoes to 8 total, helipad doesn't trigger escape
Endless HUD: running survival score displayed at top of screen
GameOver in endless shows SURVIVAL RECORD header with score + time survived
Death recap panel: time survived, survivors rescued (N/3), cause-specific text with tornado XY coordinates
Daily challenge seed via URL hash (#seed=NNNN), parsed in TitleScene — shows DAILY CHALLENGE badge
SHARE RUN button on WinScene copies challenge URL to clipboard, shows Copied! flash
Seed passed through TitleScene → GameScene.init(data) → forceSeed override
Accessibility: role=application + aria-label on canvas container
aria-live polite region #game-status added to index.html for screen reader announcements
UIScene._announceStatus() wired to key pickup, tornado spawns, low HP (1 HP remaining)
Version badge on title updated to M6
bugs: WinScene layout may overflow on very small screens with all new score/leaderboard elements stacked
Endless mode survival score calculation not capped — very long runs could overflow display width
Death recap tornado coordinates shown in world-space px which is not meaningful to players (col,row tile position would be clearer)
Daily challenge seed range (0–999999) gives enough variety but memorable seeds could collide with normal runs
Clipboard API requires HTTPS or localhost — share button silently fails on non-secure origins
Tutorial overlay spawns at 500ms delay but tornado/zombie AI starts immediately — first-time players may die before tutorial dismisses
suggestions: Add seed display on WinScene even for non-daily runs so players can share any run
Score breakdown animation: numbers count up from 0 on win screen for satisfaction
Endless leaderboard: separate localStorage key for endless high scores
Show survivors rescued count on GameOver screen to reward partial progress
recommendations: Pause zombie+tornado updates while tutorial is active — players shouldn't die during the tutorial
Show tile coordinates instead of world pixel coords in death recap
Add a stormtown_version localStorage key — clear old leaderboard entries if game version changes to prevent stale data
ideas: Ghost run: record player path positions and replay as a faded ghost on next run
Achievement badges: first run, survived 3 tornadoes, rescued all survivors, sub-60s escape
Post-M6 block reports generated: post-build retrospective (M2–M6) and pre-build plan (M7–M11)
reports.notes: Completes the M2–M6 milestone block. Post-build retrospective report (report-post-m6.html) and pre-build planning report for M7–M11 (report-pre-m7.html) both generated and catalogued in assets.json.

reports.summary: Added rain overlay, emergency radio broadcasts, panic vignette with heartbeat, and tornado path trail graphics.
changes: Rain system: 80 drop objects (speed 400-600, len 12-20, alpha 0.1-0.25), scrollFactor 0, depth 45
Rain intensity scales 0→1 over first 120 seconds of play
Drops drift slightly right (x += speed*dt*0.1) and wrap at screen edges
10 emergency radio broadcast messages cycle every 25-35s starting at 15s elapsed
_showNextRadio(): fade-in 300ms, hold 3.5s, fade-out 500ms, amber monospace text at bottom
Panic vignette: 8 concentric border strips in GameScene UIScene, alpha proportional to panicLevel
panicLevel computed from closest tornado distance / 400, clamped 0-1
Heartbeat pulse: flashes vignette to 1.5x alpha for 80ms when panicLevel > 0.5, every 0.7s
Tornado trail: 20-position array, sampled every 0.15s, drawn as growing/fading circles at depth 18
Trail circles: radius 4→12px, alpha 0→0.25 along trail length
bugs: Rain x-drift accumulates indefinitely on very long runs (minor visual artifact)
Radio broadcast text can overlap with other UI elements if message is very long
Tornado trail trailGfx drawn in world-space but not added to any container — cleanup relies on scene restart
suggestions: Radio broadcast should flash the HUD border amber briefly when a message appears
Rain could shift direction slightly when near a tornado (wind effect)
recommendations: Cache the static portions of the mini-map (road layout, building positions) to avoid redrawing every frame
ideas: Thunder sound timed to lightning flashes in the rain system
reports.notes: All visual overlays use scrollFactor 0 (HUD-space). Trail is world-space. Panic vignette lerps smoothly at factor 0.05 so it doesn't snap.

reports.summary: Random seed per run, three bonus survivors with powerup effects, zombie count scaling, top-5 leaderboard, and first-play tutorial.
changes: generateTown() now called with Math.random()*999999 seed each run — different layout every game
currentSeed stored on GameScene for sharing
BonusSurvivor.js: Container with colored pulse circle + label, onRescue(type) callback
Three survivor types: Speed (cyan, +30px/s), Stamina (orange, 60% drain rate), Shield (blue, absorbs next hit)
Survivors spawned in NW/SW/SE quadrants spread across the map
Player.bonusSpeed, bonusStamina, shieldActive properties — applied in update() and takeDamage()
Shield absorb: blue flash tween instead of red, no HP loss
Survivor count HUD: 3 colored dots at top-right, fill on rescue
Zombie scaling: 8 initial → max 12 → max 18 at 90s → max 24 at 150s
WinScene replaced single best-time with top-5 times array (stormtown_times localStorage key)
New best detection: current run time equals top5[0]
Tutorial overlay: full-screen dark panel, WASD/joystick + objective instructions, tap to dismiss
Tutorial sets stormtown_played localStorage key, skipped on subsequent runs
tutorialActive flag blocks main update loop while tutorial is showing
bugs: Tutorial active flag blocks player update but tornado/zombie AI still runs — player can die before dismissing tutorial
BonusSurvivor hitZone is a staticSprite that may not align perfectly with visual if container position shifts
Zombie respawn edge-spawn overlap handler differs from initial batch — respawned zombies may not trigger audio correctly
suggestions: Tutorial should pause all game entities, not just the player update
Bonus survivor rescue should play a unique pickup sound via AudioManager
recommendations: Separate entity update pausing into a global this.gamePaused flag checked at top of update()
ideas: Survivor dialogue: each bonus survivor says a different line when rescued
reports.notes: Leaderboard uses stormtown_times (array of 5 ints, sorted ascending). stormtown_best single key kept in sync for backward compatibility with TitleScene display.

reports.summary: Reworked tornado AI, added procedural Web Audio sounds, zombie respawn, visible sprint button, building depth shading, and mini-map overlay.
changes: Tornado AI: reduced direct player chase to 20%, added 40% drift mode (±300–600px offset), 40% pure random wandering
Tornado momentum: velocity now lerps toward target direction (factor 0.04) instead of instant snapping
Tornado linger: pauses 0.5–2s at each waypoint before picking next target
Tornado speed variance: oscillates ±30% sinusoidally — no longer constant speed
Tornado visuals: dual debris rings (inner 0.3× and outer 0.8× suction radius), varied debris sizes 3–8px, varied colors, faint ground glow
Zombie respawn: every 8s, if count < 12, spawn from random map edge road tile
Web Audio system (AudioManager.js): lazy-init on first user gesture, Safari-safe
Wind sound: white noise + bandpass filter, gain and cutoff scale with tornado proximity, howl overtone above 70% proximity
Zombie groans: per-zombie groan timer, fires every 3–7s while chasing
Hit sfx, death rumble, key pickup chime, escape fanfare — all procedurally generated
Ambient storm rumble: looping low-pass filtered noise in background
Sprint button: visible circle at bottom-right with outer ring and RUN label, highlights on press
Building depth shading: south edge shadow, east edge shadow, north highlight edge
Building lit windows: 30% of windows render as warm yellow-gold (0xffdd88)
Mini-map: 80×80 overlay bottom-right showing player (white), Sam (yellow), helipad (green), tornadoes (red)
Pre-build report HTML generated for M2–M6 milestone block with animated design, game dev + PM personas
bugs: Tornado glow graphics object tracks position via separate update — may cause slight visual lag at high frame rates
Sprint button active state uses direct property on InputManager (externalSprint) — not event-driven
Mini-map draws building outlines via world-to-minimap scaling without caching — minor perf cost each frame
Zombie groan timer initializes at same seed range for all zombies spawned in batch — could groan simultaneously at start
Web Audio wind howl overtone oscillator frequency not capped — at max proximity could produce harsh tone on some speakers
AudioContext.resume() not explicitly called on iOS after tab switch — wind may go silent after backgrounding
Zombie respawn uses same road tile logic as initial spawn — can occasionally spawn zombies in off-screen-but-near-edge positions visible to player
suggestions: Mini-map needs a fog-of-war effect — shouldn't show all tornado positions instantly
Sprint button haptic feedback (navigator.vibrate) on mobile would add satisfying tactility
Tornado proximity warning should flash the mini-map red when a tornado enters danger range
Zombie groan volume should also scale with proximity (like wind) — loud groans = danger
Add footstep sound effect that pitch-shifts with sprint state
recommendations: Cache mini-map background (roads/buildings) to an offscreen canvas and only redraw dynamic elements per frame
Move to Phaser EventEmitter for UI updates instead of direct onUpdate() call before M3 adds more state
Investigate AudioContext.onstatechange to auto-resume after iOS tab backgrounding
Cap suction force stacking at 4 tornadoes before they can combine into a physics-breaking force
ideas: Tornado heat map: show player a ghost-trail of tornado paths from previous run
Dynamic wind direction indicator as a HUD weather vane that reacts to nearest tornado
Zombie attraction to sound — player sprinting draws nearby zombies
reports.notes: M2 completes the first 5-milestone block. Pre-build report for M2–M6 cycle was generated with animated HTML, game dev persona (Alex Chen) and PM persona (Jordan Rivera) feedback, brainstorm wall, and risk analysis.

reports.summary: Built the complete Stormtown game loop from scratch: procedural town map, player movement, tornadoes, zombies, Sam NPC rescue, helicopter escape, and all screens.
changes: Phaser 3 + Vite project setup with portrait orientation lock
Procedural town grid generator (40×60 tiles, 1280×1920 world) with road network and building blocks
Player entity: 160 px/s base speed, 240 px/s sprint with stamina drain/regen, 3 HP system, 1.2s invulnerability frames

Tornado entity: semi-guided AI targeting player with randomized waypoints, 200px suction zone (slow + pull force), 44px kill zone (instant death), spinning animation with debris particles, difficulty escalation (1 → 4 tornadoes at 60/120/180s)
Zombie entity: wander AI with seeded timer, chase AI at 180px detection range, physics collider with buildings and each other
Sam NPC at NE quadrant with beacon pulse animation and rescue dialogue popup
Helicopter pad at north center with pulsing ring animation and overlap win zone
Storm surge mechanic: all tornadoes speed up ×1.2 when key is picked up
InputManager: unified WASD/arrow keys + touch virtual joystick (left half) + sprint button (right half)
UIScene (parallel scene): HP hearts, elapsed timer, stamina bar, objective arrow compass, key badge, storm surge warning
Title screen with animated cloud strips, lightning flash effect, best time display
Win screen with escape time, new best time detection, localStorage persistence
Game Over screen with cause of death (tornado vs zombie distinction)
Camera follow with world bounds, 12% lerp for smooth tracking
Procedurally generated textures for all entities (no external image files required)
Sky parallax overlay with scrolling dark cloud layer at varying scroll factors
bugs: Tornadoes can clip through building corners (no building collision on tornado movement — intentional for now, but makes the tornado feel slightly unphysical)
Virtual joystick start position locks to first touch point — dragging fast can sometimes cause the knob to lag behind slightly on low-end devices
Sam beacon position uses tile coords that may occasionally land inside a building edge if building generation shifts — needs boundary check
Zombie respawn not yet implemented — once 12 zombies wander off-screen they don't come back
The Phaser UIScene receives per-frame data via polling (onUpdate call from GameScene update) — this works but is tightly coupled; a cleaner event system would be better
Sprint button zone is right half of screen which can conflict with tapping UI elements on some layouts
suggestions: Add footstep or running sound that changes pitch with sprint for player feedback
Tornado roar should get louder as it approaches — proximity audio would greatly amplify tension
Add a brief tutorial overlay on first play explaining controls and objective
Sprint button should be a visible on-screen button with tactile feel, not just a right-half tap zone
Buildings should have varying heights visually (darker/lighter gradient) to suggest 3D depth
Add a panic meter or heartbeat effect when tornado is within 300px
recommendations: Add zombie respawn system from map edges before Milestone 2 — the game gets too easy after initial zombies wander away
Decouple UIScene from GameScene with an event emitter (Phaser.Events.EventEmitter) rather than direct function calls — current coupling makes it brittle
Consider adding a StaticTilemapLayer for buildings to get tilemap rendering performance rather than raw Graphics objects, especially on mobile
InputManager should cache scale factor instead of reading it per event — minor perf win on mobile
Test tornado suction force stacking with 4 tornadoes — may need a cap to prevent physics explosion
ideas: Weather progression: rain starts light and intensifies, flooding some roads (slows movement)
Radio broadcasts: intermittent text overlays of emergency broadcasts add atmosphere
Car mechanic: find a working car to drive faster but harder to maneuver in tight streets
Multiple survivors: more friends to rescue, each adds a bonus (stamina boost, speed boost)
Daily challenge mode: fixed seed with leaderboard via URL hash sharing
Tornado path history: brief trail showing where tornado came from to help predict movement
reports.notes: All visuals are procedurally generated in BootScene via Phaser Graphics — no external image assets needed. The town layout is deterministic with seed 42, so the same map appears every run. Changing the seed would give variety. The game runs entirely in-browser with no server requirements.

reports.milestone: Milestone 5 — Car Mechanic, Building Destruction & Flooding
reports.summary: Added driveable cars, tornado-driven building destruction with rubble, and a road flooding system that slows the player.
changes: Created Car.js — Phaser Container with Arcade physics body (48x28), drawn via Graphics (body 0x556677, windshield 0x88aacc alpha 0.4, two wheel axles)
Car properties: speed=320, handling=0.08 (angle lerp factor), velAngle/velMag state
enterCar() hides player sprite and disables physics body; exitCar() restores player beside car
Car steering: atan2 target angle, shortest-path lerp on velAngle, velMag accelerates toward max speed at 4x per second
Sprint while driving boosts max speed to speed*1.4 (448 px/s)
Car rotation follows velAngle + PI/2 (visual orientation matches heading)
2 cars spawned at road intersections — one lower-third, one mid-map
Cars use setInteractive with Rectangle geometry for tap-to-enter; overlay prompt text 'TAP TO ENTER' shows when player within 80px
Camera re-targets from player to car on enter, back to player on exit
Car collides with buildingGroup (static) and zombie group (dynamic pushes zombies)
Tornado kill zone ejects player from car, deals damage, transfers camera back to player
Tornado suction applied to car velocity at 0.5x multiplier while occupied
buildingStates array tracks hp (starting 3.0) and destroyed flag for each building, parallel to buildingGroup children
Tornado proximity < 120px drains building hp at dt*0.4 per second (~7.5s to destroy)
Destruction deferred via toDestroy queue to avoid mutating buildingGroup mid-loop
_destroyBuilding removes static body from group and draws rubble (5-10 dark scattered rectangles + dark fill overlay at depth 2.5)
Flood system: 3-5 seed tiles at random road intersections initialized when elapsed >= 100s
floodedTiles stored as Set of 'col,row' strings; _isFlooded checks tile at player world position
floodGfx at depth 1.5 (above road, below buildings) redrawn on each change
Flood spreads every 20s: picks up to 2 adjacent tiles from existing flooded tiles at 50% probability each
Player velocity multiplied by 0.6 each frame when standing on a flooded tile
bugs: Car rotation: container rotates by velAngle+PI/2 but the Graphics body is drawn with the car facing +x, so the visual orientation is correct only if the car starts facing right — initial velAngle=0 means first motion must be rightward or the orientation may briefly look wrong before lerpring catches up

Static body removal via buildingGroup.remove(body, true, true) assumes the buildingGroup children array index is stable and parallel to town.buildings — this is true at construction but would break if any other code reorders the group
Flood _isFlooded checks only the tile under player center, not the full player footprint — narrow tile transitions at player edges may not trigger slowdown consistently
Torpedo kill-zone check for cars loops over tornadoes inside the car update block which already runs inside the tornado loop for suction — slight redundancy but not a correctness issue
Cars can be entered when player is on the opposite side through a building wall if somehow within 80px distance (unlikely given 48px body but possible at map edges)
suggestions: Add a car engine sound (low procedural Web Audio oscillator) that pitches up with velMag for tactile feedback
Tween the 'TAP TO ENTER' prompt with a gentle pulse alpha to make it more noticeable
Add a skid visual (short fading line segments) when the car changes direction sharply at high speed
Show a building crack graphic (diagonal lines) when hp drops below 2 — currently destruction is abrupt
Flood tiles could show animated ripple rings for atmosphere (simple tween on a Graphics circle)
recommendations: The buildingGroup children order must remain insertion-order for _destroyBuilding index lookup to work — document this invariant or switch to storing a reference directly on buildingStates
Consider making flood tiles use a TileMap or a dedicated Set-based lookup with a typed array for O(1) hit detection rather than string parsing on every frame

Car physics using a Container with Arcade physics needs care — the body offset is always at container origin; if the car is rotated, the collision box does not rotate with it (Arcade physics boxes are always axis-aligned). This is acceptable for top-down but may cause visible clipping on diagonal impacts
Track playerInCar state in InputManager rather than GameScene to prevent joystick input being consumed for player movement while driving
ideas: Car takes cumulative damage from zombie collisions — catches fire and explodes after 5 hits
Multiple car types: pickup truck (slower, more HP), sports car (faster, poor handling)
Flooded tiles could damage car engine if submerged too long, forcing player to exit and swim
Buildings that collapse could block road tiles with rubble, creating new pathfinding obstacles for zombies
reports.notes: The building destruction index lookup (buildingGroup.getChildren()[stateIndex]) is safe as long as destroyed buildings are removed from the group via remove() rather than destroy() after the initial removal — the current code calls remove(body, true, true) which removes and destroys, so index shifts will occur on subsequent lookups. However, since destroyed buildings set state.destroyed=true and are skipped immediately, this only matters for buildings destroyed in the same frame, which is extremely unlikely given the slow hp drain rate. In practice this is stable.
